metadata.description: A platform namespace — the isolation and scheduling boundary within a cluster that workloads (VMs, containers, pods) are placed into. Models the construct that Kubernetes calls a Namespace, OpenShift calls a Project, and other platforms express as a tenant-scoped partition of cluster resources. Provider-specific because namespace semantics vary across platforms, but the role — tenant-scoped resource boundary within a cluster — is universal. A provider declares required_inputs that reference this type so placement and enrichment policies can select the right namespace for a request.

adopts.role: cluster-scoped isolation boundary for workloads and resource quotas

lifecycle.decommission: Provider deletes the namespace and all resources within it. Decommission is cascading — all contained resources must be decommissioned first or decommissioned as part of the namespace teardown.

name.description: The namespace name within the cluster (e.g. 'tenant-alpha-prod'). Unique within the containing cluster.

cluster_ref.description: The cluster this namespace exists within. Uses the canonical data-reference shape (ADR-012).

oneOf.description: Short form — handle or UUID of a Compute.Cluster.

tenant_uuid.description: The tenant this namespace is bound to. Resources placed into this namespace inherit this tenant binding.

name.description: The realized namespace name — the string value that provider_extensions fields reference.

uid.description: The platform-native UID of the namespace (e.g., Kubernetes metadata.uid).

relationships.name: cluster
relationships.description: The cluster this namespace exists within. A namespace cannot outlive its cluster.
